task_completion.description: Verify the presence of the required pools and lanes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a large pool labeled 'Service Desk' divided into three lanes labeled 'L1 Support', 'L2 Support', and 'Incident Manager'. Also look for a separate, smaller or collapsed pool labeled 'End User'.

task_completion.description: Verify the presence of the required events and tasks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a start event ('Incident Reported'), an end event ('Incident Closed'), and an intermediate timer event ('SLA Breach Warning') attached to an 'Investigate & Diagnose' task. Look for 7 tasks: 'Log Incident', 'Categorize & Prioritize', 'Apply Known Fix', 'Investigate & Diagnose', 'Implement Resolution', 'Escalate to Major Incident', and 'Close Incident'.

task_completion.description: Verify the presence of the four required exclusive gateways.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for diamond-shaped exclusive gateway shapes labeled 'Known Error?', 'L1 Resolvable?', 'Resolved?', and 'Major Incident?'.

task_completion.description: Verify the connections between elements using sequence and message flows.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for solid arrows (sequence flows) connecting tasks, gateways, and events within the 'Service Desk' pool. Look for dashed arrows (message flows) connecting the 'End User' pool to elements in the 'Service Desk' pool.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of a second page named 'Legend' containing a key.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a page tab at the bottom of the diagramming interface labeled 'Legend' and visual evidence of a key/legend explaining the diagram shapes on that page.

task_completion.description: Verify the files are saved and exported to the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for file icons on the Desktop named 'incident_management.drawio' and 'incident_management.png', or a file manager/terminal showing these files exist in the ~/Desktop directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a diagramming tool GUI (like draw.io/diagrams.net) to create the diagram.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the diagramming application interface with toolbars, shape libraries, and a canvas, rather than a text editor writing XML or terminal commands generating the file.

integrity.description: Verify the diagram was built by placing and connecting shapes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for intermediate screenshots showing the diagram in partial states of completion, with shapes being dragged, dropped, connected, and labeled.

integrity.description: Verify the PNG was exported via the application's export function.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the export dialog box in the diagramming interface or evidence that a genuine image file was created, not just a renamed text file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output files are 'incident_management.drawio' and 'incident_management.png' on the Desktop. The diagram must contain exactly 2 pools (End User, Service Desk), 3 lanes in the Service Desk pool (L1 Support, L2 Support, Incident Manager), 7 specific tasks, 4 exclusive gateways (Known Error?, L1 Resolvable?, Major Incident?, Resolved?), and 3 events (Incident Reported start, SLA Breach Warning timer, Incident Closed end). Required keywords to look for in the diagram include: incident, support, l1, l2, manager, resolve, diagnose, sla, close.
